modules.title: Set Theory, Relations, Functions, and Theorem Proving Techniques
topics: Definition of sets, countable and uncountable sets
Venn diagrams and proofs of general set identities
Definition and types of relations, composition of relations, pictorial representation
Equivalence relations and partial ordering relations; Job‑scheduling problem
Definition and types of functions, one‑to‑one, onto, into, inverse functions, composition of functions
Recursively defined functions and the pigeonhole principle
Mathematical induction and proof by contradiction

modules.title: Algebraic Structures
topics: Definition and properties of semigroups and monoids
Groups, Abelian groups, subgroup, cyclic groups, cosets, factor groups
Permutation groups, normal subgroups, homomorphism and isomorphism of groups
Rings and fields: definitions and standard results

modules.title: Propositional and First‑Order Logic
topics: Propositions, predicates, and quantifiers (universal and existential)
Basic logical operations, truth tables, tautologies and contradictions
Algebra of propositions, logical implications and equivalence
Normal forms (CNF, DNF) and logical equivalences

modules.title: Finite State Machines
topics: Finite state machines as models of physical systems
Finite state machines as language recognizers

modules.title: Graph Theory
topics: Basic terminology of graphs, planar graphs, multigraphs and weighted graphs
Isomorphic graphs, paths, cycles and connectivity
Shortest path algorithms in weighted graphs
Eulerian paths and circuits, Hamiltonian paths and circuits

modules.title: Posets, Hasse Diagrams and Lattices
topics: Properties of lattices, bounded and complemented lattices

modules.title: Recurrence Relations and Generating Functions
topics: Introduction to recurrence relations and recursive algorithms
Linear recurrence relations with constant coefficients: homogeneous and particular solutions
Generating functions and solution by the method of generating functions

reference_books: C.L. Liu, “Elements of Discrete Mathematics”, Tata McGraw‑Hill Edition.
Trembley, J.P. & Manohar, “Discrete Mathematical Structure with Application CS”, McGraw Hill.
Kenneth H. Rosen, “Discrete Mathematics and its Applications”, McGraw Hill.
Bisht, “Discrete Mathematics”, Oxford University Press.
Biswal, “Discrete Mathematics & Graph Theory”, PHI.

modules.title: Review of C and Introduction to Data Structures
topics: Operations on data structures and cost estimation
Linear data structures: linked lists – singly, circular, doubly linked
Representation of linked list in memory
Applications of linked lists (e.g., polynomial manipulation)

modules.title: Stacks and Queues
topics: Implementations of stacks (array, linked list, multiple stacks)
Applications of stacks: infix‑to‑postfix conversion, postfix evaluation, recursion
Implementations of queues (array, linked list, circular queue)

modules.title: Tree Data Structures
topics: Tree definitions – height, depth, order, degree
Binary Search Tree: operations, traversal, search
Comparative analysis of various tree types

modules.title: Graphs
topics: Graph representation techniques (adjacency matrix, adjacency list)
Graph traversal algorithms: Depth‑First Search (DFS), Breadth‑First Search (BFS)
Minimum Spanning Tree algorithms: Kruskal’s and Prim’s

modules.title: Sorting, Searching and Hashing
topics: Sorting algorithms: bubble sort, quick sort, selection sort, heap sort, insertion sort, shell sort, merge sort, radix sort
Searching techniques: sequential search, binary search
Case studies: application of data structures in operating systems and DBMS

reference_books: Aho, Hopcroft, Ullman, “Data Structures and Algorithms”, Pearson Education.
N. Wirth, “Algorithms + Data Structure = Programs”, Prentice Hall.
Jean – Paul Trembly, Paul Sorenson, “An Introduction to Structure with application”, TMH.
Richard, GilbergBehrouz, Forouzan,“Data structure – A Pseudocode Approach with C”, Thomson press.

modules.title: Number systems and Boolean algebra
topics: Review of number systems and number base conversions

modules.title: Analog‑Digital Conversion and Logic Families
topics: A/D and D/A converters and their types
Multivibrators: bistable, monostable, astable, Schmitt trigger, IC 555 and applications

experiments: To study and verify the truth tables of various Logic gates
To verify the properties of NAND and NOR gates as Universal Building Blocks.
Simplification and implementation of a Boolean function
Implementation of basic Boolean arithmetic logic circuits such as Half‑adder, Half‑subtractor, Full adder and Full subtractor
Conversion from Binary to Gray and Gray to Binary code
To construct a binary multiplier using combinational logic and to verify with the truth table
To verify 2‑bit Magnitude comparator for all possible conditions
Generation of various logical functions using 8‑to‑1 multiplexer
Construction of a 4‑bit ripple counter and study of its operation
Operation of IC‑555 Timer as Monostable, Astable and Bistable multivibrators
To characterize binary ladder type digital to analog (D/A) and analog to digital (A/D) converter
Design and implementation of various types of flip‑flops using JK flip‑flop
To study natural sampling of continuous time waveforms using different sampling rates
To study Pulse‑Code modulation with Time‑division multiplexing (PCM‑TDM)
To study generation and detection of BPSK and QPSK waveforms
reference_books: Morris Mano, Digital Circuits & Logic Design, PHI
Gothman, Digital Electronics, PHI
Tocci, Digital Electronics, PHI
Mavino & Leach, Digital Principles & Applications, PHI
Taub and Schilling, Digital Integrated electronics.
Simon Haykin, Introduction to Analog & Digital Communication, Wiley.
Lathi B.P., Modern analog & digital communication, Oxford University.

modules.title: Introduction to Object Oriented Thinking & Programming
topics: Features of Object oriented paradigm – merits and demerits

modules.title: Encapsulation and Data Abstraction
topics: State, behavior and identity of an object

reference_books: G. Booch, “Object Oriented Analysis & Design”, Addison Wesley.
James Martin, “Principles of Object Oriented Analysis and Design”, Prentice Hall/PTR.
Peter Coad and Edward Yourdon, “Object Oriented Design”, Prentice Hall/PTR.
Herbert Schildt, “Java 2: The Complete Reference”, McGraw-Hill Osborne Media, 7th Edition.
